danger_signals.visual_form: NPC faction uniforms and armor variants; talking head character models during dialogue
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies hostile entities and aggressive factions in settlements and wilderness

danger_signals.visual_form: Characters refusing dialogue or initiating combat animations
danger_signals.meaning: Negative reputation threshold crossed; player is unwelcome or marked as enemy

danger_signals.visual_form: Post-nuclear wasteland aesthetic; ruins and collapsed structures
danger_signals.meaning: World state: catastrophic nuclear war occurred 164 years prior; danger persists in environment

danger_signals.visual_form: NPC dialogue responses change; characters shun or refuse interaction
danger_signals.meaning: Player actions have damaged standing; increased hostility risk in faction territories

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs initiate friendly dialogue; quest opportunities displayed via talking head conversations
safety_signals.meaning: Player is accepted; faction members offer aid, quests, and trading opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Populated towns with multiple NPCs; dialogue options available
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; diplomatic interaction possible; questing hub

safety_signals.visual_form: Fourteen available companion characters with distinct talking head models
safety_signals.meaning: Player can form alliances; companions support exploration and combat

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs exchange goods; gold coins used as currency faction-produced
safety_signals.meaning: Stable economic structures; faction control of trade indicates settlement security

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Faction-specific NPC uniforms and armor visible in 3D talking head models
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Settlement control indicated by NPC population composition and dialogue tone
Reputation slowly decays if player delays first main quest; visual cue: NPC dialogue becomes cooler
Presence indicated by prevalence of gold coin currency in settlements; economic control signal

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-nuclear catastrophe (164 years after atomic war)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trimetric isometric perspective shows ruined landscapes, collapsed buildings, wasteland aesthetics across the West Coast of United States
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs reference survival, factions compete for resources, settlements are sparse and fortified

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player reputation with faction/settlement is positive
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPCs display friendly talking head models; dialogue is receptive and warm
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs offer quests, trading, companions; new quest lines open; no combat initiated on sight

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player reputation with faction/settlement is negative
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPCs display hostile talking head animations; dialogue becomes cold or unavailable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs refuse to work with player; may turn hostile on sight; quest opportunities blocked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Small village setting; limited NPC variety; hometown reputation visible in dialogue warmth
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reputation decay timer runs while player delays; NPCs become progressively less friendly

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-main-story (13 in-game years available before engine limit)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All discovered locations remain accessible; no visual lockout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continued exploration and side quest completion possible; no story-driven time pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dialogue responses from NPCs shift based on player action history
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Future story opportunities and ending variations determined by cumulative karma; NPCs treat player accordingly

environmental_storytelling.example: Trimetric isometric landscapes show collapsed infrastructure, radiation-damaged areas, makeshift settlements built from pre-war remnants; communicates world has not recovered 164 years after nuclear war
Different settlements populated by faction members with distinct visual uniforms and armor; shows territorial control and political fragmentation across the wasteland

3D talking head character models vary in appearance and demeanor; friendly NPCs show welcoming expressions, while hostile factions display contempt or fear; conveys political tension without exposition

environmental_storytelling.visual_example: NPC dialogue tone and availability shifts in response to player karma and faction standing; visual cue delivered through dialogue text and character model expressions rather than explicit meters

environmental_storytelling.example: Multiple quest solutions (diplomacy, combat, stealth, unconventional) allow player to navigate political factions; completion method affects karma and future faction standing; shows factions have competing interests

Fourteen distinct companion characters with unique talking head models suggest varied factions and survivors; their availability and willingness to join reflects player's reputation across different groups

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Initiate dialogue; offer quests and trading; display friendly talking head expressions; recruit as companions
Refuse dialogue; turn hostile on sight; refuse trading and recruitment; shun player in public
Offer limited dialogue; may be convinced through charisma or barter; no quests available until reputation shifted
Reputation slowly decreases; dialogue becomes cooler; receptiveness diminishes over time
Grant rewards; open new quest lines; improve player reputation visibly through dialogue warmth
Join exploration and combat; behavior configurable via menu; respond to combat scenarios with assigned tactics
Exchange goods using faction gold coins; demonstrate economic stability of faction controlling that settlement

notes: Fallout 2 uses isometric trimetric perspective and dialogue-driven NPC interaction to convey faction identity and political standing. The reputation system is the primary manifestation layer for world-state: it directly controls NPC behavior, quest availability, and story outcomes without requiring explicit UI meters. Faction identity is communicated through NPC uniforms, settlement composition, and currency types rather than explicit color/sigil systems. The 164-year post-apocalyptic setting is established through environmental ruins and thematic NPC dialogue rather than dynamic weather or time-of-day shifts. Karma system creates moral world-state that alters NPC treatment and story branches. No explicit danger/safety HUD elements documented; communication is entirely through NPC behavior, dialogue tone, and environmental presence.
